previous_statement: (Step 2: Extremality implies face bound). Since φ is an extreme point of C, dim(F_C(φ)) = 0. By Node 1.2 (Weis-Shirokov, with K = S(A) and m affine constraints f_j(ψ) = ψ(a_j) = β_j): dim(F_{S(A)}(φ)) ≤ dim(F_C(φ)) + m = 0 + m = m.
new_statement: (Step 2: Extremality implies face bound — amended). Since φ is an extreme point of C, by definition F_C(φ) = {φ}, hence dim(F_C(φ)) = 0. By Node 1.3.1, the hypotheses of Node 1.2 (Weis-Shirokov) are satisfied with K = S(A) compact convex and f_j(ψ) = ψ(a_j) continuous affine. Applying Node 1.2.3 (extremal specialisation of Weis-Shirokov) with m equality constraints f_j(ψ) = β_j: dim(F_{S(A)}(φ)) ≤ dim(F_C(φ)) + m = 0 + m = m. The same argument applies to inequality constraints φ(a_j) ≤ β_j or mixed constraints, since Node 1.2 covers sublevel sets C = {x ∈ K : f(x) ≤ β} as well as level sets (see Node 1.3.5 for the mixed case). This is a deduction depending on Nodes 1.2 (specifically 1.2.3), 1.3.1, and the definition of extreme point.
